mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate earnings, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between INDPRO changes and forward 20-day SPX returns is less than 0.2, or the Rank IC over a 3-year period is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 3% year-on-year, the central bank is pressured to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the average forward 20-day SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in the labor market (rising UNRATE) and consumer spending (falling RSAFS) indicates a synergistic demand contraction that amplifies negative earnings expectations, causing large negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising over 3 months and RSAFS falling over 2 months
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the median forward 60-day SPX return is not below the unconditional median, or the joint signal has no predictive accuracy (Sharpe ratio < 0) over two or more recessions.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary easing cycle, a decline in the federal funds rate compresses discount rates and boosts equity valuations within a five-day window as markets anticipate accommodative policy.
regime_hint: federal funds rate trending downward for at least 3 months
failure_condition: In identified easing cycles, the average forward 5-day return after FEDFUNDS reductions is not positive, or the signal's information ratio is below 0.2 over the last three easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor demand, rising income, and consumer confidence, pushing S&P 500 higher over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When initial payroll gains exceed 200,000, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (Rank IC < 0.05 over a 2-year period).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and an increase in the federal funds rate indicates tightening financial conditions that directly impair housing-sensitive sectors and broad equity valuations, producing negative returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline and FEDFUNDS month-over-month increase
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing weakness
failure_condition: The joint signal shows no significant negative average forward return (or positive 60% of instances) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate output and corporate profitability, mechanically boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading day horizon as earnings revisions climb.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between real GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.1 over a 10-year period, or the signal has no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail and food services sales exceeding 1% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending, which typically precedes negative earnings surprises and equity market declines within a week.
failure_condition: In events where RSAFS drops more than 1% month-over-month, the median forward 5-day SPX return is not negative, or the strategy has a negative Sharpe ratio over a 5-year period.
